# Linux Default Password Change

passwd  
*\# Change the password of current user*

sudo passwd \[username\]  
*\# Change password for other user*

- [ ] ## Backup User

*Debian/Ubuntu*  
sudo adduser \[username\]  
*\# Creates a new user*

*Debian/ubuntu*  
sudo usermod \-aG sudo \[username\]  
*\# This add the \[username\] to sudo group*

*Arch/Red Hat/Fedora/CentOS/Oracle*  
sudo useradd \-m \[username\]  
passwd \[username\]  
*\# Creates a new user and set the password*

*Arch/Red hat/Fedora/CentOS*  
sudo usermod \-aG wheel \[username\]  
*\# Add the \[username\] to sudo group*

- [ ] ## Backup Services

mkdir \[backup location\]  
cd /  
sudo tar \-cpf \[backup-name.tar\] /etc  
sudo cp \[backup-name.tar\] / \[backup location\]  
*\# Copies backup to a different location*

/usr/bin  
/usr/sbin  
/opt (if splunk look at addendum)  
/srv (if not empty)  
*\# Other Backup Places*

# Linux Discovery

*su root and less/cat each file*

- [ ] ### Network(ips)       	ip a \> ips

      	\# *Find ip and mac address saves to file*  
      	ip r \> route  
      	\# *Enter Gateway*

- [ ] ### Services/ports

      	ss \-autpn \> ports  
      	\# *Note and mark down ports open and service is listed*

- [ ] ### Nmap

      	nmap \-sV \-T4 \-p- localhost \> nmap  
      *\# Run and scan the machines and save the file. Note any concerning ports to follow up on*

- [ ] ### Users

      	getent group \> groups  
      	\# *User is sudo/wheel group*

- [ ] ### Firewall

      firewall-cmd \--list-all-zones \> firewall  
      iptables-save \> firewall  
      *\# This give list of all current settings to see weird ones and save*

- [ ] ### Process check looking for active rev shell

      	ps \-eaf \--forest \> pss  
      	cat /etc/profile  
      	ls /etc/profile.d/  
      	\~/.bashrc

      \~/.bashrc.d  
      \~/.bash\_profile  
      *\# Check for revshells*  
      *\# Note any suspicious and check*

- [ ] ### Cron check for active rev shells

      crontab \-l  
      	crontab \-l \-u \[user\]  
      	cat /etc/crontab  
      	\# *list crontabs*  
      	*\# note any that need to be checked*

- [ ] ### Binary/Package Check

      *Debian/Ubuntu*

      apt update && apt install \-y debsums 

      (debsums \-s \> debsums\_results.txt 2\>&1) &

      *\# Scans for changed or missing package files*

*Red Hat/Fedora/CentOs/Oracle*  
rpm \-Va \> rpm\_results.txt 2\>&1 &  
*\# Scans for changed or missing package fill*

# Linux Addendum

### AIDE (File Integrity Checks)

*Debian/Ubuntu*  
apt install aide  
aide \-i \--config /etc/aide/aide.config \> aidebackup &  
mv /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new /var/lib/aide/aide.db  
*\# Install*  
*Arch/Red Hat/Fedora/CentOS/Oracle*  
dnf install aide  
aide \-i \--config /etc/aide.config \> aidebackup &  
mv /var/lib/aide.db.new.gz /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz  
*\# Install*

*All*  
less /var/log/aide/aide.log  
*\#\# If you can’t see all of the output from the previous command, you can run the following*

*Debian/Ubuntu*  
aide \-C \-c /etc/aide/aide.conf  
*\#\# Missing configuration? Run aide \-C in combination with the \-c option*  
*Arch/Red Hat/Fedora/CentOS/Oracle*  
aide \-C \-c /etc/aide.conf  
*\#\# Missing configuration? Run aide \-C in combination with the \-c option*

*Debian/Ubuntu*  
aide \-u  
mv /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new /var/lib/aide/aide.db  
*\#\# If all file changes are legitimate, run this to update the database*  
*Arch/Red Hat/Fedora/CentOS/Oracle*  
aide \-u  
mv /var/lib/aide.db.new.gz /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz  
*\#\# If all file changes are legitimate, run this to update the database*

### Apache

*debian/ubuntu*  
sudo apt update && sudo apt install apache2  
\# *Install Apache web server*

*debian/ubuntu*  
sudo systemctl start apache2  
*\# Start the Apache service*

*debian/ubuntu*  
sudo systemctl enable apache2  
*\# Enable Apache to start on boot*  
*red hat/fedora/centos/oracle*  
sudo dnf install httpd  
*\# Install Apache web server*

*red hat/fedora/centos/oracle*  
sudo systemctl start httpd  
*\# Start the Apache service*

*red hat/fedora/centos/oracle*  
sudo systemctl enable httpd  
*\# Enable Apache to start on boot*

*All*  
sudo tar \-cpf ht.tar /var/www/html  
sudo cp ht.tar / \[backup location\]  
*\# Backup Website. Also check /opt if not file in other location*

*debian/ubuntu*  
ls /etc/apache2/sites-enabled  
Confirm website doesn’t have /phpmyadmin  
*\# Check for phpmyadmin be sure to remove*  
*red hat/fedora/centos/oracle*  
ls /etc/httpd/sites-enabled  
Confirm website doesn’t have /phpmyadmin  
*\# Check for phpmyadmin be sure to remove*

### 

### Auditd Setup

apt install auditd audispd-plugins | dnf install audit  
nano /etc/audit/rules.d/99-custom.conf  
\# Now enter the following lines inside of the 99-custom.conf file. You don’t need to include any of the comments

\-a always,exit \-F arch=b64 \-S creat,open,openat,truncate,ftruncate \-F exit=-EACCES \-F auid\>=1000 \-F auid\!=unset \-k access  
\-a always,exit \-F arch=b64 \-S creat,open,openat,truncate,ftruncate \-F exit=-EPERM \-F auid\>=1000 \-F auid\!=unset \-k access  
\-a always,exit \-F arch=b32 \-S creat,open,openat,truncate,ftruncate \-F exit=-EACCES \-F auid\>=1000 \-F auid\!=unset \-k access  
\-a always,exit \-F arch=b32 \-S creat,open,openat,truncate,ftruncate \-F exit=-EPERM \-F auid\>=1000 \-F auid\!=unset \-k access  
*\# Unsuccessful file access attempts **(key: access)***

\-w /etc/group \-p wa \-k identity  
\-w /etc/passwd \-p wa \-k identity  
\-w /etc/shadow \-p wa \-k identity  
\-w /etc/pam.conf \-p wa \-k identity  
\-w /etc/pam.d \-p wa \-k identity  
*\#\# Modifications to user/group info **(key: identity)***

\-a always,exit \-F arch=b64 \-S chmod,fchmod,fchmodat,chown,fchown,lchown,fchownat \-F auid\>=1000 \-F auid\!=unset \-F key=perm\_mod  
\-a always,exit \-F arch=b32 \-S chmod,fchmod,fchmodat,chown,fchown,lchown,fchownat \-F auid\>=1000 \-F auid\!=unset \-F key=perm\_mod  
\-a always,exit \-F arch=b64 \-S setxattr,lsetxattr,fsetxattr,removexattr,lremovexattr,fremovexattr \-F auid\>=1000 \-F auid\!=unset \-F key=perm\_mod  
\-a always,exit \-F arch=b32 \-S setxattr,lsetxattr,fsetxattr,removexattr,lremovexattr,fremovexattr \-F auid\>=1000 \-F auid\!=unset \-F key=perm\_mod  
*\#\# Modifications to ownership, group, permissions, attributes **(key: perm\_mod)***

\-w /var/run/utmp \-p wa \-k session  
\-w /var/log/wtmp \-p wa \-k session  
\-w /var/log/btmp \-p wa \-k session  
*\#\# Track session creations **(key: session)***

\-w /var/log/lastlog \-p wa \-k logins  
\-w /var/run/faillock \-p wa \-k logins  
*\#\# Track successful and failed logins **(key: logins)***

\-a always,exit \-F arch=b64 \-S unlink,unlinkat,rename,renameat \-F auid\>=1000 \-F auid\!=unset \-k delete  
\-a always,exit \-F arch=b32 \-S unlink,unlinkat,rename,renameat \-F auid\>=1000 \-F auid\!=unset \-k delete  
*\#\# Track file deletions **(key: delete)***

\-a always,exit \-F path=/usr/sbin/usermod \-F perm=x \-F auid\>=1000 \-F auid\!=unset \-k priv\_comm  
\-a always,exit \-F path=/usr/bin/passwd \-F perm=x \-F auid\>=1000 \-F auid\!=unset \-k priv\_comm  
\-a always,exit \-F path=/usr/bin/sudo \-F perm=x \-F auid\>=1000 \-F auid\!=unset \-k priv\_comm  
\-a always,exit \-F path=/usr/bin/su \-F perm=x \-F auid\>=1000 \-F auid\!=unset \-k priv\_comm  
\-a always,exit \-F path=/usr/sbin/useradd \-F perm=x \-F auid\>=1000 \-F auid\!=unset \-k priv\_comm  
\-a always,exit \-F path=/usr/sbin/userdel \-F perm=x \-F auid\>=1000 \-F auid\!=unset \-k priv\_comm  
*\#\# Track usage of tools requiring elevated permissions **(key: priv\_comm)***

augenrules \--load  
*\# Save and close the file, then run*

### Auditd Usage

aureport  
aureport \--help  
*\# To get a quick overview of audit logs & get help on all possible reports you can view*

\# Useful reports  
aureport \-i \-e  
*\# Events in general*  
aureport \-i \-k  
*\# Events but based on keys*  
aureport \-i \--comm  
*\# Commands run*

aureport \-i \-f  
*\# File interactions*  
aureport \-i \-u  
*\# User actions*

aureport \-i \-k  
*\# User modification*

aureport \-i \-p  
*\# Actions by process IDs*

aureport \-i \-x  
*\# Executables ran*

aureport \-i \-s  
*\# Syscalls*

aureport \-i \-tm  
*\# Terminals*  
ausearch \-a \<event ID\>  
*\# If you want more specific information, take note of the event ID in any of the reports, and use this command*  
*\# ausearch can also be used to get more specific searches, and see more specific information about audit logs.*

### Backup and Restore section

sudo tar \-cpf \[backup-name.tar\] \[folder\]  
*\# backup or tar a file*  
sudo tar \-xpf backup.tar  
*\# restore or untar a file*

### Binary/Package Check

*Debian/Ubuntu*  
sudo apt update  
sudo apt install \-y debsums  
sudo debsums \-s  
*\# Scans for changed or missing package files*

*Red Hat/Fedora/CentOs/Oracle*  
sudo ls  
sudo rpm \-Va  
*\# Scans for changed or missing package files*

### 

### Cockpit

*Red Hat/Fedora/CentOs/Oracle*  
systemctl stop cockpit  
systemctl disable cockpit  
dnf remove cockpit  
*\# Stop and remove it always*

*Firewall blocking income tcp/9090*

### Cron jobs

crontab \-l  
crontab \-l \-u \[user\]  
cat /etc/crontab  
*\# list crontabs*	  
crontab \-e  
crontab \-e \-u \[user\]  
vim /etc/crontab  
*\# edit crontabs only comment out*

cat /etc/profile  
ls /etc/profile.d/  
\~/.bashrc  
\~/.bashrc.d  
\~/.bash\_profile  
ls /etc/bash\_completion.d  
ls /etc/pam.d  
*\# Check for revshells*

### DNS

Edit /etc/resolve.conf  
Add  
nameserver 8.8.8.8  
nameserver 8.8.4.4  
*\# Temporary force of dns, wipes on reboot*

### Groups

getent group  
getent group \[username\]  
*\# groups with users. Check wheel/sudo*  
cat /etc/group  
*\# Shows groups with :users*

groups  
*\# print current user groups*  
id \[username\]  
*\# Shows user group, useful to know primary group*

### IP and Gateway

ip a  
*\# Get Ip and Mac Address*  
ip r  
*\# Get gateway/route*

*Newer*  
nmcli device show  
*\# Get ip, mac, and gateway/route*  
*Older*  
ifconfig or route \-n  
*\# Get ip, mac, and gateway/route*

### 

### Login banner

Edit /etc/ssh/sshd\_config  
Add  
Banner /etc/issue.net  
*\# Maybe uncomment.*  
*\# This allows ssh to use text in /etc/issues.net*

Edit or Create /etc/issue.net  
Place Banner text  
Edit or Create /etc/issue  
Place Banner text  
systemctl restart sshd if needed  
Take a screenshot of banner  
*\# This allows for text when logout in kernel then login*

### Mariadb/MySQL

mysql \-u \[username\] \-p \-e "SHOW DATABASES; EXIT;"  
\# list databases

mysqldump \-u \[username\] \-p \[database\_name\] \> \[database\_name\]\_backup.sql  
mysqldump \-u \[username\] \-p \[database\_name table\_name\] \> \[table\_name\]\_backup.sql  
\# Backup sql

mysql \-u username \-p \[database name\] \< \[database file\].sql  
mysql \-u username \-p \[database name\] \< \[table file\].sql  
\# New Copy from Backup, must use different name

mysql \-u username \-p \-e "DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS \[name\]; CREATE DATABASE \[name\];"  
mysql \-u username \-p \[name\] \< \[database file\].sql  
mysql \-u username \-p \-e "USE \[database name\]; DROP TABLE IF EXISTS \[table name\];"  
mysql \-u username \-p \[database name\] \< \[table name\].sql  
\# Overwrite from Backup

sudo mysql\_secure\_installation  
*\# Used to set root password, will need to update applications settings*

mysql \-u root \-p \-e "USE mysql; ALTER USER 'root'@'%' IDENTIFIED BY 'new\_secure\_password';"  
\# Change user password, will need to update applications settings. % means any host.

mysql \-u root \-p \-e "SELECT User, Host FROM mysql.user;"  
\# Show users

mysql \-u root \-p \-e "CREATE USER 'new\_user'@'localhost' IDENTIFIED BY 'user\_password';"  
\# Add User

Firewall Notice, either block incoming tcp 3306 or only allow between machines that need it

### Nmap

*Debian/Ubuntu*  
sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade \-y  
sudo apt install nmap \-y  
*\# install nmap*  
*Fedora/Centos*  
sudo dnf upgrade \--refresh \-y  
sudo dnf install nmap \-y  
*\# install nmap*

nmap \-sV \-T4 \-p- \[ip/localhost\] \> nmap.txt  
*\# Run and scan the machines and save the file*

### 

### Port Scan ss and netstat

sudo ss \-autpn  
*\# List all listening TCP/UDP ports with process info*

sudo netstat \-tulnp  
*\# List all listening TCP/UDP ports with process info*  
ss \-tuln  
sudo ss \-tan  
*\# Show only listening tcp sockets, and show all tcp socket*  
sudo ss \-uan  
*\# Show UDP sockets*

sudo ss \-tulnp | grep :\[port number\]  
*\# Check specific port*  
Lsof \-i :\[port number\]  
*\# Useful for some process more hidden*

### Process List ps

ps \-eaf | less  
*\# List all running processes*  
ps \-eaf \--forest | less  
*\# Show process tree with hierarchy and relationships*

ps \-u \[username\]  
*\# Show processes running for a specific user*

### Process killing

Kill \-9 \[pid\]  
*\# This force kills a process*

### Services

systemctl list-units \--type=service \--state=running  
*\# List all running services*

systemctl list-units \--type=service  
*\# List all loaded services*  
systemctl list-unit-files \--type=service \--state=enabled  
*\# List all enabled services that start on boot*

systemctl list-units \--type=service | grep cockpit  
*\# search services*

### Ssh

**Backup**  
**Removeing**  
format all section more of backup, remove/secure, firewall, backup  
*Ubuntu/Debian*  
apt remove openssh-server  
*\# Remove SSH if not scored or being used*

Firewall drop incoming port 22 tcp  
*Red Hat/Fedora/CentOs/Oracle*  
dnf remove openssh-server  
sudo systemctl daemon-reload  
*\# Remove SSH if not scored or being used*  
*How to Enable*  
*Update*  
*add note to firewall of check firewall section*  
**Secure**  
**\# how to secure the SSH**  
**Firewall,Backup**

### sudoers

sudo visudo  
*\# Shows sudoers config. Check for ALL=(ALL:ALL) ALL. Usually at bottom*

sudo cat /etc/sudoers  
sudo cat /etc/sudoers.d/\*  
*\# Check for ALL=(ALL:ALL) ALL. Make sure to check README in sudoers.d*

### Splunk Server or Indexer

wget [https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BYU-CCDC/public-ccdc-resources/refs/heads/main/splunk/splunk.sh](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BYU-CCDC/public-ccdc-resources/refs/heads/main/splunk/splunk.sh)  
Comment out lines 825 and 826 to fix HTTPS issue

./splunk.sh \-i  
Set user: splunk  
Splunk Logs: /opt/splunk/var/log/splunk  
*\# Install Indexer/Server*

./splunk.sh \-i  
Restart after install  
systemctl start/stop/status Splunkd.service  
*\# start stop when have systemd managed*

Changing webui password can be done in the webui  
*\# Changing password*

### 

### Splunk Forwarder or client/agent

wget [https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BYU-CCDC/public-ccdc-resources/refs/heads/main/splunk/splunk.sh](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BYU-CCDC/public-ccdc-resources/refs/heads/main/splunk/splunk.sh)  
./splunk.sh \-f \[Indexer IP\]  
Set user: splunkfwd Set password the same as the printer user password of the host  
*\# Install Forwarder/Client*

./splunk.sh \-i  
Restart after install  
*\# Reinstall*  
*\# Changing process user password is easiest with a reinstall.*  
./splunk.sh \-D  
*\# Debug only forwarder*

/opt/splunk/bin/splunk start/stop/status  
*\# Start, stop, or status*

./splunk.sh \-a \[path\]  
*\# Add a new file for splunk to monitor and send logs from, it’ll ask for an index to assign it to, options are main, web, linux, snoopy, system, misc.*

### Suricata

*Debian/Ubuntu*  
sudo apt update && sudo apt install \-y suricata  
*\# Install Suricata*

*Red Hat/Fedora/CentOS/Oracle*  
sudo dnf install \-y epel-release  
sudo dnf install \-y suricata  
*\# Install Suricata (epel-release needed for repo)*

Edit /etc/suricata/suricata.yaml  
Set HOME\_NET to your network range  
vars:  
  address-groups:  
    HOME\_NET: "\[172.16.0.0/12\]"  
*\# Change this to match your actual network*  
Edit /etc/suricata/suricata.yaml  
Set the interface under af-packet section  
af-packet:  
  \- interface: eth0  
*\# Change eth0 to your actual interface. Use "ip a" to find it*

sudo suricata-update  
*\# Download ET Open rules. Run this first before starting*

sudo suricata-update list-sources  
sudo suricata-update enable-source \[source-name\]  
sudo suricata-update  
*\# List available rulesets, enable one, then update again*

sudo systemctl enable \--now suricata  
*\# Start and enable on boot*  
sudo suricata \-T \-c /etc/suricata/suricata.yaml  
*\# Test config for errors without starting*

/var/log/suricata/fast.log  
\# Quick one-line alert log, easiest to read

/var/log/suricata/eve.json  
\# Full JSON log with all detail (alerts, dns, http, tls, flow)

/var/log/suricata/stats.log  
\# Performance and packet stats

grep "ET MALWARE" /var/log/suricata/fast.log  
\# Search for malware signature hits

grep "ET TROJAN" /var/log/suricata/fast.log  
\# Search for trojan signature hits

grep "ET SCAN" /var/log/suricata/fast.log  
\# Search for scan/recon activity

### Zeek

*Debian/Ubuntu*  
sudo apt update && sudo apt install \-y zeek  
*\# Install Zeek*  
*If not in default repos:*  
*echo 'deb http://download.opensuse.org/repositories/security:/zeek/xUbuntu\_24.04/ /' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/zeek.list*  
*curl \-fsSL https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/security:/zeek/xUbuntu\_24.04/Release.key | gpg \--dearmor | sudo tee* /etc/apt/trusted.gpg.d/zeek.gpg \> /dev/null  
ppt update

*Red Hat/Fedora/CentOS/Oracle*  
sudo dnf install \-y zeek  
*\# Install Zeek*  
*If not in default repos:*  
*sudo dnf install \-y 'dnf-command(copr)'*  
*sudo dnf copr enable @zeek/zeek*

sudo vim /opt/zeek/etc/node.cfg  
Change the line:  
interface=eth0  
*\# Edit node config and set your sniffing interface to your actual interface. Use "ip a" to find it*

sudo nano /opt/zeek/etc/networks.cfg  
Example:  
192.168.1.0/24    Private subnet  
10.0.0.0/8        Internal range  
*\# Define your local networks, one per line*  
sudo /opt/zeek/bin/zeekctl deploy or start or stop  
\# Start Zeek and begin monitoring  
sudo /opt/zeek/bin/zeekctl status or diag  
\# Check if running

/opt/zeek/logs/current/  
*\# Current active logs (rotated periodically)*

/opt/zeek/logs/current/conn.log  
*\# Every connection: IPs, ports, duration, bytes. MOST IMPORTANT LOG*

/opt/zeek/logs/current/dns.log  
*\# Every DNS query and response with domain names*

/opt/zeek/logs/current/http.log  
*\# HTTP requests: URLs, user agents, methods*

/opt/zeek/logs/current/ssl.log  
*\# TLS/SSL connections: certs, SNI (domain over HTTPS)*

/opt/zeek/logs/current/files.log  
*\# Files seen on the wire: type, size, hashes*

/opt/zeek/logs/current/weird.log  
*\# Protocol violations and unusual behavior*

/opt/zeek/logs/current/notice.log  
*\# Zeek's built-in alerts and notice*

grep \-i "suspicious-domain.com" /opt/zeek/logs/current/dns.log  
Search for specific domain in DNS logs  
grep "10.0.0.50" /opt/zeek/logs/current/conn.log  
Search all connections involving a specific IP  
grep \-E "\\b443\\b" /opt/zeek/logs/current/conn.log | less  
Show all HTTPS connections  
grep \-E "\\b(4444|5555|8080|1337|9001)\\b" /opt/zeek/logs/current/conn.log  
Search for common suspicious ports in connections  
grep \-v "dns" /opt/zeek/logs/current/conn.log | grep \-E "\\b53\\b"  
Find traffic on port 53 that isn't DNS (possible DNS tunneling)  
Beacon Hunting with conn.log  
cat /opt/zeek/logs/current/conn.log | zeek-cut id.orig\_h id.resp\_h id.resp\_p duration orig\_bytes resp\_bytes | sort | less  
Review connections with duration and byte counts  
Look for: repeated connections to same dest IP, similar durations, similar byte sizes  
cat /opt/zeek/logs/current/conn.log | zeek-cut id.resp\_h | sort | uniq \-c | sort \-rn | head \-20  
Top 20 most connected-to destination IPs. Beacons will have high counts  
cat /opt/zeek/logs/current/conn.log | zeek-cut id.orig\_h id.resp\_h | sort | uniq \-c | sort \-rn | head \-20  
Top 20 source-destination IP pairs by connection count  
zeek-cut is Zeek's built-in column extraction tool. If zeek-cut is not found use:  
/opt/zeek/bin/zeek-cut  
Or add /opt/zeek/bin to PATH as shown in setup

### Snoopy

snoopyctl  
*\# Logs to /var/log/snoopy.log*

snoopyctl conf  
/etc/snoopy.ini  
*\# Config at one of those*

### Users

getent passwd  
*\# shows user information. More modern. Look for user with /bin/bash or similar*  
cat /etc/passwd  
*\# shows user information*

**SIEM Agent**  
**NFS/SMB**  
**Debian Bind Appromer fix note**  
**Debian 9 fixing packages look for sed**  
**Centos package fix look for sed**  
**Phpmyadmin remove stuff**  
**Prestashop fix**  
Password (Update ps\_employee set passwd \= md5(‘\<cookie\>more-secure-with-this’) where id\_employee \= 1;)  
Update admin password  
Cp settings change settings.inc.php file  
Mv to sql file  
cd /  
tar \-cf ettc etc/  
cd /var/  
tar \-cf wwww www/  
mysqldump \-p prestashop \> p.sql  
(password just press enter)  
Mysqldump \-p mysql \> my.sql  
**Prestashop docker rebuild**  
**Disabling PHP modules in setting.inc**  
**Swaks**  
This is in fedora way to test emails  
**Dovecot(mostly backup stuff)**  
**Postfix(mostly backup stuff)**  
**FTPS**  
**FTP**  
**WAF(Web Application Firewall)**  
**LibreNMS**

# Windows

- [ ] ## First attempt to Run Hayden’s script

*\#Hayden’s script needs internet access*

Use this site to find the command to run Hayden’s script  
[https://github.com/SUU-Cybersecurity-Club/CCDC\_Bible/blob/main/ccdc-bible.md](https://github.com/SUU-Cybersecurity-Club/CCDC_Bible/blob/main/ccdc-bible.md)  
Open Notepad and paste/type command there, then copy to powershell  
*\# Use Copy Paster in 2 sections*  
*\# Scroll a bit down. Use the copy paste script to put in the first 2 lines first, then paste the rest. (it has issues doing the whole thing at once)*  
*\# Additionally bible only has 1 cd windows-hardening need 2 cd windows-hardening*

*\# If the copy paste does not work for this, type in this command directly instead.*  
\[Net.ServicePointManager\]::SecurityProtocol \= \[Net.SecurityProtocolType\]::Tls12;  
Invoke-WebRequest https://github.com/SUU-Cybersecurity-Club/CCDC-Scripts/releases/latest/download/windows-hardening.zip \-Outfile windows-hardening.zip;  
Expand-Archive \-Path windows-hardening.zip \-DestinationPath windows-hardening;  
cd windows-hardening  
cd windows-hardening  
.\\start.bat

**Check for Errors. Especially windows defender (AV).**  
*\# There are a lot of issues that can crop up when running Hayden’s script: please refer to the section in the addendum about common issues. If it still does not work after trying to fix it for 15 minutes or so, please refer to the other parts of the addendum to perform its process manually.*

***Only run these if Hayden’s Script fails***

\# **run the following in powershell ISE on desktop servers. If on a core machine (CMD) run them one at a time from a powershell prompt (to do so, type in powershell in the cmd to start a powershell terminal).**

- [ ] ## Default Password Change

*\# These are the commands, in powershell, to change the password for Windows users*

$Password \= Read-Host “please enter password” \-AsSecureString  
Set-LocalUser \-Name “\[user\]” \-Password $Password  
*\# **local user** password change format*

$Password \= Read-Host “please enter password” \-AsSecureString  
Set-LocalUser \-Name “Administrator” \-Password $Password  
*\# **local admin** password change*

Set-ADAccountPassword \-Identity \[username\] \-Reset \-NewPassword \`  
(Read-Host \-AsSecureString "please enter password")  
*\# **Active Directory (AD) account** password change*  
	\# For the AD commands the “ \` ” at the end of the first line is necessary.

Set-ADAccountPassword \-Identity Administrator \-Reset \-NewPassword \`  
(Read-Host \-AsSecureString "please enter password")  
*\# **AD admin account** password change*

- [ ] ## Backup User

\# adding backup user on both AD and local  
*AD admin user*  
$Password \= Read-Host "Enter password for the new admin account" \-AsSecureString

New-ADUser \`  
    \-Name "printer" \`  
    \-SamAccountName "printer" \`  
    \-UserPrincipalName "printer@\[domain-name\]" \`  
    \-AccountPassword $Password \`  
    \-Enabled $true \`  
    \-Path "OU=Admins,DC=domain,DC=local"

Add-ADGroupMember \-Identity "Domain Admins" \-Members "printer"

*local admin user*  
$Password \= Read-Host "Enter password for local user" \-AsSecureString

New-LocalUser \`  
    \-Name "printer" \`  
    \-Password $Password \`  
    \-FullName "printer" \`  
    \-Description "extra admin account"

Add-LocalGroupMember \`  
    \-Group "Administrators" \`  
    \-Member "printer"

- [ ] ## Backup services

*\#because of the way that Windows works, there is no single directory for you to backup, like there is in Linux.* 

# 

# Windows Discovery

### Network(ips) \- see addendum section: IP and Gateway

*\#there are commands to find all adapters, mark gateway and local ip, grabs domain too*

### Windows defender installed \- see addendum section: AV errors/ install win defender

*\#If it errored on hayden’s script on the set-mppreference section, then windows defender must be installed.*

### Services/ports \- see addendum section: Nmap

### Login banner \- see addendum section: login banner

### Users(local/admin, service users, admins, remote desktop, etc.) \- see addendum section: users

### Smb \- see addendum section: smb

### Firewall \- done (are firewall and defender on?) \- see addendum section: firewall

### NTP C2 Beacons \- 

# Windows Addendum

### Hayden’s script common issues

* The URL did not find the script

  *\# If this is the case, you may be running the wrong command to download and execute it. Please run the command listed in this document at the top, and this URL should be active. If not, then you will need to do Hayden’s script steps manually. To do so, please see the steps to Hayden’s script section.*

* You see transcript errors  
  	\# These won’t stop the script, they are simply there to help log the script actions.

### Defender/Anti Virus Errors

**![][image1]**  
*\# Fix by Installing windows defender*

### Defender Install

Install-WindowsFeature Windows-Defender  
Restart  
Now rerun ./start.bat  
*\# Once you install this. Restart the machine. Re-run script. Will have to enter passwords again*

*AV commands from script*  
Get-MpComputerStatus  
*\#This command tells you the status of many of the windows defender parts on the computer*

Start-Process powershell "echo 'Updating AV signatures...'; Update-MpSignature"

echo "\`nSetting protections on..."  
Set-MpPreference \-MAPSReporting Advanced  
Set-MpPreference \-SubmitSamplesConsent Always  
Set-MpPreference \-DisableBlockAtFirstSeen 0  
Set-MpPreference \-DisableIOAVProtection 0  
Set-MpPreference \-DisableRealtimeMonitoring 0  
Set-MpPreference \-DisableBehaviorMonitoring 0  
Set-MpPreference \-DisableScriptScanning 0  
Set-MpPreference \-DisableRemovableDriveScanning 0  
Set-MpPreference \-PUAProtection Enabled  
Set-MpPreference \-DisableArchiveScanning 0  
Set-MpPreference \-DisableEmailScanning 0  
Set-MpPreference \-CheckForSignaturesBeforeRunningScan 1  
*\#use these commands if you don’t want to rerun the whole start.bat script*

### Hayden’s Debugging Links

[https://github.com/SUU-Cybersecurity-Club/CCDC-Scripts/blob/main/windows-hardening](https://github.com/SUU-Cybersecurity-Club/CCDC-Scripts/blob/main/windows-hardening)

[https://github.com/SUU-Cybersecurity-Club/CCDC-Scripts/releases/latest/download/windows-hardening.zip](https://github.com/SUU-Cybersecurity-Club/CCDC-Scripts/releases/latest/download/windows-hardening.zip)

[https://github.com/SUU-Cybersecurity-Club/CCDC\_Bible/blob/main/ccdc-bible.md](https://github.com/SUU-Cybersecurity-Club/CCDC_Bible/blob/main/ccdc-bible.md)

### IP and Gateway

ipconfig /all  
\# *Show ip, gateway and domain connected to.*

### Nmap

Get-NetTCPConnection  
*\# The official command to use for powershell to find nmap info*

*server 2022 and later*  
winget install \--id Insecure.Nmap \-e  
*\# after installing, it should run as nmap usually runs on linux*  
*2019 and before*  
*\# You will need to operate without nmap, there is no native way to install it from powershell.*

### Login banner

Set-ItemProperty \-Path "HKLM:\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\System" \-Name "legalnoticecaption" \-Value $title  
Set-ItemProperty \-Path "HKLM:\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\System" \-Name "legalnoticetext" \-Value $message  
Or   
Open RegEdit  
Goto to HKEY\_LOCAL\_MACHINE \> SOFTWARE \>Microsoft \> Windows \> CurrentVersion \> Policies \> System  
Click just system. Edit legalnoticecaption and legalnoticetext  
![][image2]  
*\# this displays a legal notice banner for potential inject stuffs. For these you will need to put in your own message if doing it manually.*

### 

### Users

(local/admin, service users, admins, remote desktop, etc.)  
net localgroup administrators  
*\# This will show everything that can access administrator privileges*

net localgroup users  
*\# This will show local users*

Get-ADGroupMember "Domain Admins" \-Recursive |  
Select Name, SamAccountName, ObjectClass

Get-ADGroupMember "Enterprise Admins" \-Recursive |  
Select Name, SamAccountName, ObjectClass  
*\# On the domain controller, two groups operate primarily as admins, domain admins and enterprise admins. To see who is in which group*

### SMB

Set-SmbServerConfiguration \-EnableSMB1Protocol $false \-Force;  
Set-SmbServerConfiguration \-EnableSMB2Protocol $false \-Force  
*\# SMB disable*

### Windows firewall

Set-NetFirewallProfile Domain,Public,Private \-Enabled True  
\#*this turns on the firewall*

### Anonymous Login Group

**![][image3]**  
*Change these keys to reflect this:  RestrictAnonymous=1, RestrictAnonymousSAM=1, EveryoneIncludesAnonymous=0*  
*\#if the group policy does not work, you can edit these registry keys on the individual machine to fix anonymous logon*

### Kerberos preauth

**![][image4]**

### Backup files and folders in powershell

*Single file:*  
Compress-Archive \-Path "\[absolute path to file\]" \-DestinationPath "\[absolute output path\].zip"

*Directory:*  
Compress-Archive \-Path "\[absolute path to directory\]" \-DestinationPath "\[absolute output path\].zip"

*Time-stamped backup command example:*  
$Date \= Get-Date \-Format "yyyy-MM-dd\_HHmm"  
Compress-Archive \-Path "C:\\Logs" \-DestinationPath "C:\\Backups\\Logs\_$Date.zip"

*How to save a list of the services running on a win server*  
$BackupPath \= "C:\\Service\_Backups"  
New-Item \-ItemType Directory \-Path $BackupPath \-Force

Get-CimInstance Win32\_Service |  
Select-Object \`  
    Name,  
    DisplayName,  
    State,  
    StartMode,  
    StartName,  
    PathName |  
Export-Csv "$BackupPath\\Services\_Backup.csv" \-NoTypeInformation

### Dsrm password

*DSRM set new password (run each line separate from one another)*  
ntdsutil  
set dsrm password  
reset password on server null  
Quit  
Quit  
*\# after the third line, it should prompt for the new password*

### Splunk Forwarder

curl [https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BYU-CCDC/public-ccdc-resources/refs/heads/main/splunk/splunk.ps1](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BYU-CCDC/public-ccdc-resources/refs/heads/main/splunk/splunk.ps1) \-o splunk.ps1  
.\\splunk.ps1 \-ip \[Server IP\]  
\# USERNAME is “splunk” Use PASSWORD for the printer user of that host  
REBOOT  
.\\splunk.ps1 \-ResetPassword \# Use if you aren’t able to log in. Default user is always “splunk”

\# Download splunk install script and run it with the IP of the splunk server, then reboot

cd to Splunk Home: C:\\Program Files\\SplunkUniversalForwarder\\bin  
.\\splunk add monitor "C:\\File\\To\\Monitor0" \-index main  
\# Add new file for splunk to monitor, index options are, *main, web, windows, system, misc.*

### 

### Suricata

Download and install Npcap from https://npcap.com/  
*\# IMPORTANT: Check "WinPcap API-compatible mode" during install*  
*Suricata needs Npcap to capture packets. Without WinPcap mode it will not start*

Download the .msi installer from https://suricata.io/download/  
Run suricata-\<version\>.msi and follow the installer Next \> Next  
 *\# Default install location: C:\\Program Files\\Suricata*

Find your interface name  
Open CMD as Administrator  
ipconfig /all  
Note the full name of your network adapter (e.g. "Ethernet0" or "Wi-Fi")  
This is the interface you will tell Suricata to monitor  
Edit suricata.yaml  
Open C:\\Program Files\\Suricata\\suricata.yaml in Notepad++ or similar  
Set HOME\_NET to your network range  
vars:  
address-groups:  
HOME\_NET: "\[172.16.0.0/12\]"  
*\# Change this to match your actual network*

Set the interface under the pcap section (Windows uses pcap not af-packet)  
pcap:  
\- interface: Ethernet0  
*\# Change Ethernet0 to your actual interface name from ipconfig /all*

If suricata-update works on your version:  
Open CMD as Administrator  
cd "C:\\Program Files\\Suricata"  
suricata-update  
Manually download ET Open rules from:  
https://rules.emergingthreats.net/open/suricata-7.0/emerging.rules.tar.gz  
Pick the closest version to your Suricata install  
Extract and copy the .rules files into C:\\Program Files\\Suricata\\rules\\  
*\# May or may not work on Windows depending on version. Manual download is the safe option*

Open CMD as Administrator  
cd "C:\\Program Files\\Suricata"  
suricata.exe \-T \-c suricata.yaml  
Test config for errors without starting  
suricata.exe \-c suricata.yaml \-i "Ethernet0"  
*\# Start Suricata on your interface. Replace Ethernet0 with your interface name*

Keep this CMD window open, Suricata runs in the foreground  
To run in background as a service (if supported by your version):  
sc create Suricata binPath= ""C:\\Program Files\\Suricata\\suricata.exe" \-c "C:\\Program Files\\Suricata\\suricata.yaml" \-i "Ethernet0" \--service" start= auto  
net start Suricata  
*\# Create and start a Windows service. Not all versions support \--service flag*

C:\\Program Files\\Suricata\\log\\fast.log  
*\# Quick one-line alert log, easiest to read*

C:\\Program Files\\Suricata\\log\\eve.json  
*\# Full JSON log with all detail (alerts, dns, http, tls, flow)*

C:\\Program Files\\Suricata\\log\\stats.log  
*\# Performance and packet stats*

C:\\Program Files\\Suricata\\log\\suricata.log  
*\# Suricata's own startup and error log. Check here first if something is wrong*

Reading Logs (CMD)  
type "C:\\Program Files\\Suricata\\log\\fast.log"  
Print all alerts  
type "C:\\Program Files\\Suricata\\log\\fast.log" | find "ET MALWARE"  
Search for malware signature hits  
type "C:\\Program Files\\Suricata\\log\\fast.log" | find "ET TROJAN"  
Search for trojan signature hits  
type "C:\\Program Files\\Suricata\\log\\fast.log" | find "ET SCAN"  
Search for scan/recon activity  
type "C:\\Program Files\\Suricata\\log\\fast.log" | find "10.0.0.50"  
Search for specific IP in alerts  
Reading Logs (PowerShell \- better for eve.json)  
Get-Content "C:\\Program Files\\Suricata\\log\\fast.log" \-Tail 20  
Show last 20 alerts  
Get-Content "C:\\Program Files\\Suricata\\log\\fast.log" \-Wait  
Watch alerts in real time (like tail \-f on Linux)  
Get-Content "C:\\Program Files\\Suricata\\log\\fast.log" | Select-String "MALWARE"  
Search for malware hits  
Get-Content "C:\\Program Files\\Suricata\\log\\eve.json" \-Wait | ConvertFrom-Json | Where-Object { $\_.event\_type \-eq "alert" }  
Watch alerts from eve.json in real time  
Get-Content "C:\\Program Files\\Suricata\\log\\eve.json" | ConvertFrom-Json | Where-Object { $\_.event\_type \-eq "dns" } | Select-Object \-ExpandProperty dns | Group-Object rrname | Sort-Object Count \-Descending | Select-Object \-First 20  
Top 20 most queried DNS domains. Good for finding beacons  
Get-Content "C:\\Program Files\\Suricata\\log\\eve.json" | ConvertFrom-Json | Where-Object { \_.event\_type \-eq "alert" } | Select-Object timestamp, src\_ip, dest\_ip, dest\_port, @{N='signature';E={  
\_.alert.signature}}  
Clean alert summary with IPs, ports, and signature name  
Get-Content "C:\\Program Files\\Suricata\\log\\eve.json" | ConvertFrom-Json | Where-Object { \_.event\_type \-eq "tls" } | Select-Object src\_ip, dest\_ip, @{N='sni';E={  
\_.tls.sni}}

### Wireshark

*\#to get interface name*  
tshark \-D

tshark \-i "Ethernet 2" \-T fields \-e frame.time \-e ip.src \-e ip.dst \-e tcp.dstport \-e dns.qry.name \-e http.host \-e tls.handshake.extensions\_server\_name \-w capture.pcap

**GPO ( gpedit.msc )**  
Password Policy ( Comp Config \> Windows \> Security \> Account \> Password )  
Minimum 12 characters, complexity enabled, max 60 days  
Lockout Policy  ( Comp Config \> Windows \> Security \> Account \> Account Lockout)  
 Threshold \= 5, duration \= 5,   
Logon / Logoff Audit  
\-  Account Logon (Comp Config \> Wind Set \> Sec Set \> Adv Audit policy \> audit pol \> Acc Logon   
Enable Success and Failure for Kerberos Authentication  
Enable Success and Failure for Service Ticket Operations  
\- Logon/Logoff   (Comp Config \> Win Set \> Sec Set \> Adv Audit pol \> audit pol \> Acc Logon \>logon/logoff   
Enable Success and Failure for Audit Logon  
Enable Success for Audit Special Logon  
**SMB and see if there are any shares that will allow anonymous**  
**how to stop Zerologon**  
**secure connections (NTLJ, smbv.2)**  
**verify services**  
**automate updates**  
**Klist tickets**  
run kilist in ps or klist tickets  
shows cached tickets  
use to check for extra tickets that can be exploited

### 

### Sysmon script

*\#This script checks all the network connections that sysmon logs and collects only SSH connections. It then stores those in a file \#named sysmon-ssh-logs.txt. To adjust it, change the port number to any port you want to, and it can show any logs it has on that \#port. See part 2 to understand how to log more than only one port at a time.*

\# Output file path  
$OutputFile \= "C:\\sysmon-ssh-logs.txt"

\# Get Sysmon Event ID 3 events  
$Events \= Get-WinEvent \-FilterHashtable @{  
    LogName \= "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational"  
    Id      \= 3  
} \-ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

\# Filter for SSH (Destination Port 22\)  
$SSHEvents \= $Events | Where-Object {  
    $\_.Message \-match "DestinationPort:\\s+22"  
}

\# Write results  
$SSHEvents | Select-Object TimeCreated, Id, Message |  
    Format-List | Out-File \-FilePath $OutputFile \-Encoding UTF8

Write-Output "SSH connection logs written to $OutputFile"

*\#Part 2:*  
*\#Doing more uses an “or” flag to detect two options. This can be done both with purely ports or any \#part of the message of the log. \#The following script checks for both ssh and udp ports:*  
*\#The script after it also checks for ssh and udp, but does so differently:*

\# Output file path  
$OutputFile \= "C:\\sysmon-network-logs.txt"

\# Get Sysmon Event ID 3 events  
$Events \= Get-WinEvent \-FilterHashtable @{  
    LogName \= "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational"  
    Id      \= 3  
} \-ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

\# Filter for SSH (Port 22\) OR UDP protocol  
$FilteredEvents \= $Events | Where-Object {  
    $\_.Message \-match "DestinationPort:\\s+22" \-or  
    $\_.Message \-match "DestinationPort:\\s+53"  
}

\# Write results  
$FilteredEvents | Select-Object TimeCreated, Id, Message |  
    Format-List | Out-File \-FilePath $OutputFile \-Encoding UTF8

Write-Output "SSH and UDP connection logs written to $OutputFile"

—----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

\# Output file path  
$OutputFile \= "C:\\sysmon-network-logs.txt"

\# Get Sysmon Event ID 3 events  
$Events \= Get-WinEvent \-FilterHashtable @{  
    LogName \= "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational"  
    Id      \= 3  
} \-ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

\# Filter for SSH (Port 22\) OR UDP protocol  
$FilteredEvents \= $Events | Where-Object {  
    $\_.Message \-match "DestinationPort:\\s+22" \-or  
    $\_.Message \-match "Protocol:\\s+udp"  
}

\# Write results  
$FilteredEvents | Select-Object TimeCreated, Id, Message |  
    Format-List | Out-File \-FilePath $OutputFile \-Encoding UTF8

Write-Output "SSH and UDP connection logs written to $OutputFile"

### Windows Update Reg Repair

sc.exe create wuauserv binPath= "C:\\Windows\\System32\\svchost.exe \-k netsvcs \-p"  
sc.exe config wuauserv type= share  
sc.exe config wuauserv start= delayed-auto  
sc.exe config wuauserv DisplayName= "Windows Update"

reg add "HKLM\\OFFLINE\\ControlSet001\\Services\\wuauserv" /v ImagePath /t REG\_EXPAND\_SZ /d "%systemroot%\\system32\\svchost.exe \-k netsvcs \-p" /f  
reg add "HKLM\\OFFLINE\\ControlSet001\\Services\\wuauserv" /v Start /t REG\_DWORD /d 3 /f  
reg add "HKLM\\OFFLINE\\ControlSet001\\Services\\wuauserv" /v Type /t REG\_DWORD /d 32 /f  
reg add "HKLM\\OFFLINE\\ControlSet001\\Services\\wuauserv" /v ObjectName /t REG\_SZ /d "LocalSystem" /f  
reg add "HKLM\\OFFLINE\\ControlSet001\\Services\\wuauserv" /v DisplayName /t REG\_SZ /d "Windows Update" /f  
reg add "HKLM\\OFFLINE\\ControlSet001\\Services\\wuauserv\\Parameters" /v ServiceDll /t REG\_EXPAND\_SZ /d "%systemroot%\\system32\\wuaueng.dll" /f  
reg add "HKLM\\OFFLINE\\ControlSet001\\Services\\wuauserv\\Parameters" /v ServiceDllUnloadOnStop /t REG\_DWORD /d 1 /f  
reg add "HKLM\\OFFLINE\\ControlSet001\\Services\\wuauserv\\Parameters" /v ServiceMain /t REG\_SZ /d "WUServiceMain" /f

Then restart

sc.exe create WinDefend binPath= "\\"C:\\Program Files\\Windows Defender\\MsMpEng.exe\\""  
sc.exe config WinDefend type= own  
sc.exe config WinDefend start= auto  
sc.exe config WinDefend DisplayName= "Windows Defender Antivirus Service"  
sc.exe config WinDefend depend= RpcSs  
reg add "HKLM\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\WinDefend" /v ImagePath /t REG\_EXPAND\_SZ /d "\\"%ProgramFiles%\\Windows Defender\\MsMpEng.exe\\"" /f  
reg add "HKLM\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\WinDefend" /v Start /t REG\_DWORD /d 2 /f  
reg add "HKLM\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\WinDefend" /v Type /t REG\_DWORD /d 16 /f  
reg add "HKLM\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\WinDefend" /v ObjectName /t REG\_SZ /d "LocalSystem" /f  
reg add "HKLM\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\WinDefend" /v DisplayName /t REG\_SZ /d "Windows Defender Antivirus Service" /f  
reg add "HKLM\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\WinDefend" /v ErrorControl /t REG\_DWORD /d 1 /f  
reg add "HKLM\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\WinDefend" /v DependOnService /t REG\_MULTI\_SZ /d "RpcSs" /f

Or if offline  
reg load HKLM\\OFFLINE D:\\Windows\\System32\\config\\SYSTEM

reg add "HKLM\\OFFLINE\\ControlSet001\\Services\\WinDefend" /v ImagePath /t REG\_EXPAND\_SZ /d "\\"%ProgramFiles%\\Windows Defender\\MsMpEng.exe\\"" /f  
reg add "HKLM\\OFFLINE\\ControlSet001\\Services\\WinDefend" /v Start /t REG\_DWORD /d 2 /f  
reg add "HKLM\\OFFLINE\\ControlSet001\\Services\\WinDefend" /v Type /t REG\_DWORD /d 16 /f  
reg add "HKLM\\OFFLINE\\ControlSet001\\Services\\WinDefend" /v ObjectName /t REG\_SZ /d "LocalSystem" /f  
reg add "HKLM\\OFFLINE\\ControlSet001\\Services\\WinDefend" /v DisplayName /t REG\_SZ /d "Windows Defender Antivirus Service" /f  
reg add "HKLM\\OFFLINE\\ControlSet001\\Services\\WinDefend" /v ErrorControl /t REG\_DWORD /d 1 /f  
reg add "HKLM\\OFFLINE\\ControlSet001\\Services\\WinDefend" /v DependOnService /t REG\_MULTI\_SZ /d "RpcSs" /f

reg unload HKLM\\OFFLINE

# Firewall

- [ ] ## Default Password Change

*Palo Alto*  
Device \> Administrators \> click \[username\] \> change password form \> Then Commit  
![][image5]  
*\# This only to change administrator passwords needed for admin, see check local users below*

*Palo Alto*  
Device \> Local User Database \> Users \> click \[username\] \> edit user form \> Then Commit  
![][image6]  
*\# Change local user password, or enable/disable*

*Cisco FTD*  
Admin/Username(Top right) \> menu Profile \> Password  
![][image7]  
*\# Update Cisco FTD admin password*

- [ ] ## Backup User

*Palo Alto*  
Device \> Administrators \> click \[username\] \> click **add** in bottom right \> Then Commit  
![][image8]  
*\# In administrator can add backup user*

- [ ] ## Remove Management Profiles

- [ ] ## Backup Services

### Palo Alto

Policies \> Security \> bottom area click PDF/CSV export  
*\# All pages can be saved with this, use for injects*

Device \> Setup\> Operations \> Export named configuration snapshot \> running-config.xml  
*\#Export firewall configs*

Device \> Setup \> Operations \> Import named configuration snapshot \> \[config file\]  
*\#Import/restore firewall configs*

### Cisco

Device \> Device Administration \> Download Configuration  
*\# Download config in JSON format*

Device \>  Backup and Restore \> Configure  
*\# Create Backup*

Device \> Backup and Restore \> Upload  
*\# Upload Backup File*

### Device \> Setup \> Services Update Server: updates.paloaltonetworks.com Primary NTP Server: 0.pool.ntp.org

*\# Configure DNS and NTP Servers*

### 

# Firewall Discovery

- [ ] ### Users

Palo Alto:  
Device \> Administrators/Users  
	*\# Delete/Disable Extra Users or Administrators | users is further down the page*

- [ ] ### Interfaces

Palo Alto:  
Network \> Interfaces/Interface Mgmt  
Cisco:  
	Device \> Interfaces

- [ ] ### Nat

Palo Alto:  
Policies \> Nat

- [ ] ### Rules

Palo Alto:  
Policies \> Security   
	\#look at rules to see what it is doing

- [ ] ### The Management Permission

Palo Alto:  
Device \> Administrators  
*\# Look at roles to see permissions* 

# Firewall Addendum

### Palo Alto

Source: untrust \> Destination: trust \> Application/Service  \> Actions: Allow/Deny/Drop  
*\# Inbound, Application/Service is what you want the rule to apply to*

Source: trust \> Destination: untrust \> Application/Service  \> Actions: Allow/Deny/Drop  
*\# Outbound, Application/Service is what you want the rule to apply to*

Source: Address \> Destination: Any \> Application/Service: Any  \> Actions: Deny/Drop  
*\# Block IP*

Network \> Network Profiles \> Interface Mgmt \> Add \> add IP addresses to allow  
*\#Restrict access to Palo Alto Management Web Interface*

### Cisco

Source \> zone \> outside\_zone | Destination \> zone \> inside\_zone  
*\#Inbound*

Source \> zone \> inside\_zone | Destination \> zone \> outside\_zone  
*\#Outbound*

Source Networks \> create new network, enter IP, set rule to BLOCK  
*\# Blocking IPs*

Device \> System Settings \> Management Access \> Management Interface \> add IPs to Allowed Networks  
*\# Restrict access to Cisco Management Web Interface*

*Palo Alto*  
*![][image9]*  
*![][image10]*  
*\# Sample Firewall Rules*

*Cisco*  
*![][image11]*  
*\# Sample Firewall Rules*

### VyOs

configure  
set system login user \[username\] authentication plaintext-password \[new-password\]  
commit  
save  
*\# Set User Password*

*\# Add User*

### *W*indows Firewall

Set-NetFirewallProfile Domain,Public,Private \-Enabled True  
\# *this turns on the firewall*

*Generic block tcp port rule*  
New-NetFirewallRule \`  
    \-DisplayName "Block TCP 445 Inbound" \`  
    \-Direction Inbound \`  
    \-Protocol TCP \`  
    \-LocalPort 80 \`  
    \-Action Block  
*\#change the protocol to udp to block a udp port*  
*\#if you want to block a range of ports, change the localport value to something like this: 5000-6000*

*Block outbound traffic*  
New-NetFirewallRule \`  
    \-DisplayName "Block TCP 443 Outbound" \`  
    \-Direction Outbound \`  
    \-Protocol TCP \`  
    \-RemotePort 443 \`  
    \-Action Block  
*\#important to note that this uses a different flag than the above one. It changes Localport to Remoteport*  
*Block only a certain address*  
New-NetFirewallRule \`  
    \-DisplayName "Block RDP from 192.168.1.50" \`  
    \-Direction Inbound \`  
     \-RemoteAddress 192.168.1.50 \`  
    \-Action Block  
    \-LocalPort 3389 \`  
    \-Protocol TCP \`

*Remove a rule*  
Remove-NetFirewallRule \-DisplayName "\[rule name\]”  
*All firewall rules*  
Get-NetFirewallRule  
*Only certain info from rules*  
Get-NetFirewallRule |  
Select-Object DisplayName, Enabled, Direction, Action, Profile

### Iptables

iptables-save  
*\# Only generates text of all iptables. Useful to see all tables meaning all rules.*  
iptables \-L \-n \-v  
*\# List default table in numeric format and verbose*

iptables \-t \[table\] \-L \-n \-v  
*\# List table in numeric format and verbose*

iptables \-L \--line-numbers  
*\# List all rules with their current line numbers (needed for inserting/deleting by number)*

iptables \-P \[chain\] \[DROP or ACCEPT\]  
*\# Command to set default chain action. So default drop or accept*

iptables \-A INPUT \-p tcp \--dport 22 \-j ACCEPT  
iptables \-A INPUT \-i lo \-j ACCEPT  
iptables \-P INPUT DROP  
*\# Example of using drop input. First add rules for needed income(include ssh if over ssh/22). Add loopback. Then default drop all.*  
iptables \-A \[chain\] \-p \[tcp/udp\] \[--dport/--sport\] \[port\] \-j \[ACCEPT/REJECT/DROP\]  
*\# Creating rules to chain with protocol, destination port, source port, accept or reject*  
iptables \-A INPUT \-m conntrack \--ctstate ESTABLISHED,RELATED \-j ACCEPT  
*\# Essential rule: allows return traffic for connections you initiated*

iptables \-A \[chain\] \[rule\]  
*\# Append rule to the end of a specific chain*

iptables \-I \[chain\] \[rule\]  
*\# Insert rule at the very top (position 1\) of a specific chain*

iptables \-I \[chain\] \[number\] \[rule\]  
*\# Insert rule at a specific line number (shifting others down)*

iptables \-R \[chain\] \[number\] \[new\_rule\]  
*\# Replace an existing rule at a specific number with a new definition*

iptables \-D \[chain\] \[number\]  
*\# Delete a rule by its specific line number*

iptables \-A INPUT \-s \[IP/subnet\] \-j DROP  
*\# Silently discard all packets from a specific source without sending a response*

iptables \-A INPUT \-i \[interface\] \-j ACCEPT  
*\# Allow all traffic entering through a specific hardware interface (e.g., eth0)*

iptables \-A INPUT \-s \[IP\_ADDRESS\] \-j DROP  
*\# Block all traffic from a specific source IP address*

iptables \-t nat \-A POSTROUTING \-o \[interface\] \-j MASQUERADE  
*\# Common NAT rule to allow internal traffic to share an external IP*  
iptables \-F  
*\# Flush all rules in the current table (clears the configuration)*

apt install iptables-persistent  
*\# Install the package to automate rule loading on boot*

netfilter-persistent save  
*\# Save current runtime rules to /etc/iptables/rules.v4 (persistent storage)*  
netfilter-persistent start  
*\# Load saved rules into the kernel*

systemctl enable netfilter-persistent  
*\# Ensure the service starts automatically at system boot*

### ufw

ufw reset  
*\# Flush all rules and disable firewall (clears the configuration)*

ufw enable  
*\# Enable UFW and ensure it starts automatically at system boot*  
ufw disable  
*\# Disable UFW*

ufw reload  
*\# Reload the firewall to apply changes made to configuration files*

ufw status verbose  
*\# Only generates text of all ufw rules. Useful to see status and active rules.*

ufw status numbered  
*\# List all rules with their current line numbers (needed for deleting by number)*  
ufw show raw  
*\# List raw netfilter rules in numeric format and verbose*

ufw default \[allow/deny/reject\] \[incoming/outgoing/routed\]  
*\# Command to set default chain action. So default deny or allow*

ufw allow 22/tcp  
ufw allow in on lo  
ufw default deny incoming  
*\# Example of using deny input. First add rules for needed income (include ssh/22). Add loopback. Then default deny all.*  
ufw \[allow/deny/reject\] \[in/out\] \[proto tcp/udp\] \[from address\] \[to address\] port \[port\]  
*\# Creating rules with direction, protocol, source/destination, and port*

ufw allow eth0  
*\# Essential rule: UFW automatically handles ESTABLISHED,RELATED traffic once enabled.*

ufw allow \[rule\]  
*\# Append rule to the end of the existing rule list*

ufw insert 1 \[rule\]  
*\# Insert rule at the very top (position 1\) of the rule list*  
ufw insert \[number\] \[rule\]  
*\# Insert rule at a specific line number (shifting others down)*

ufw delete \[number\]  
*\# Delete a rule by its specific line number*

ufw deny from \[IP/subnet\]  
*\# Silently discard all packets from a specific source without sending a response*

ufw allow in on \[interface\]  
*\# Allow all traffic entering through a specific hardware interface (e.g., eth0)*  
ufw deny from \[IP\_ADDRESS\]  
*\# Block all traffic from a specific source IP address*

### 

### nft nfttable

nft list ruleset  
*\# Only generates text of all nftables. Useful to see all tables meaning all rules.*

nft list table \[family\] \[table\]  
*\# List table in numeric format and verbose (e.g., nft list table inet my\_table)*  
nft list ruleset \-n  
*\# List table in numeric format and verbose*

nft list table \[family\] \[table\] \-n \-a  
*\# List all rules with their current handles (needed for inserting/deleting by specific handle)*

nft add chain \[family\] \[table\] \[chain\] { type filter hook \[hook\] priority 0 ; policy \[drop/accept\] ; }  
*\# Command to set default chain action. So default drop or accept*

nft add rule \[family\] \[table\] INPUT tcp dport 22 accept  
nft add rule \[family\] \[table\] INPUT iif lo accept   
nft add chain \[family\] \[table\] INPUT { policy drop ; }  
*\# Example of using drop input. First add rules for needed income. Add loopback. Then default drop all.*

nft add rule \[family\] \[table\] \[chain\] \[ip/ip6\] \[protocol\] \[dport/sport\] \[port\] \[accept/reject/drop\]  
*\# Creating rules to chain with protocol, destination port, source port, accept or reject*

nft add rule \[family\] \[table\] \[chain\] ct state established,related accept  
nft add rule inet filter input ct state established,related accept  
nft add rule inet filter output ct state established,related accept  
*\# Essential rule: allows return traffic for connections you initiated*

nft add rule \[family\] \[table\] \[chain\] \[rule\]  
*\# Append rule to the end of a specific chain*  
nft insert rule \[family\] \[table\] \[chain\] \[rule\]  
*\# Insert rule at the very top (position 1\) of a specific chain*

nft insert rule \[family\] \[table\] \[chain\] index \[number\] \[rule\]  
*\# Insert rule at a specific index (shifting others down)*

nft replace rule \[family\] \[table\] \[chain\] handle \[number\] \[new\_rule\]  
*\# Replace an existing rule at a specific handle with a new definition*

nft delete rule \[family\] \[table\] \[chain\] handle \[number\]  
*\# Delete a rule by its specific handle number*

nft add rule \[family\] \[table\] INPUT saddr \[IP/subnet\] drop  
*\# Silently discard all packets from a specific source without sending a response*

nft add rule \[family\] \[table\] INPUT iifname "\[interface\]" accept  
*\# Allow all traffic entering through a specific hardware interface (e.g., eth0)*

nft add rule \[family\] \[table\] INPUT saddr \[IP\_ADDRESS\] drop  
*\# Block all traffic from a specific source IP address*

nft flush ruleset  
*\# Flush all rules in the current table (clears the configuration)*

nft list ruleset \> /etc/nftables.conf  
*\# Save current runtime rules to configuration file (persistent storage)*  
systemctl start nftables  
*\# Load saved rules into the kernel*

systemctl enable nftables  
*\# Ensure the service starts automatically at system boot*

### 

### firewalld

firewall-cmd \--list-all-zones  
*\# Only generates text of all configurations for all zones. Useful to see all rules.*

firewall-cmd \--list-all  
*\# List default zone configuration in a human-readable format.*  
firewall-cmd \--zone=\[zone\] \--list-all  
*\# List specific zone configuration.*

firewall-cmd \--get-active-zones  
*\# List all currently active zones and the interfaces assigned to them.*

firewall-cmd \--set-default-zone=\[zone\]  
*\# Command to set the default zone (e.g., public, drop, or block).*

firewall-cmd \--permanent \--add-port=22/tcp  
firewall-cmd \--permanent \--add-service=ssh  
firewall-cmd \--reload  
*\# Example of allowing incoming. Add port or service, then reload to apply. Default behavior depends on the zone's target.*  
firewall-cmd \--zone=\[zone\] \--add-port=\[port\]/\[tcp/udp\]  
*\# Creating rules for a specific zone with protocol and port.*

firewall-cmd \--add-\[service/port\]  
*\# Append rule to the runtime configuration (immediate but temporary).*

firewall-cmd \--permanent \--add-\[service/port\]  
*\# Add rule to the permanent configuration (requires reload to take effect).*

firewall-cmd \--direct \--add-rule ipv4 filter INPUT 0 \[rule\]  
*\# Insert rule at a specific priority using the direct interface (advanced).*

firewall-cmd \--permanent \--zone=\[zone\] \--remove-service=\[service\]  
*\# Replace/Modify is usually done by removing the old entry and adding the new one.*

firewall-cmd \--permanent \--remove-port=\[port\]/tcp  
*\# Delete a specific rule by removing its definition.*

firewall-cmd \--permanent \--add-rich-rule='rule family="ipv4" source address="\[IP/subnet\]" drop'  
*\# Silently discard all packets from a specific source using a rich rule.*

firewall-cmd \--zone=\[zone\] \--add-interface=\[interface\]  
*\# Assign a specific hardware interface to a zone to control its traffic.*

firewall-cmd \--permanent \--add-rich-rule='rule family="ipv4" source address="\[IP\_ADDRESS\]" reject'  
*\# Block all traffic from a specific source IP address using a rich rule.*

firewall-cmd \--permanent \--zone=public \--add-masquerade  
*\# Common NAT rule to allow internal traffic to share an external IP (masquerading).*

firewall-cmd \--panic-on / firewall-cmd \--panic-off  
*\# "Flush" alternative: Panic mode cuts all incoming/outgoing packets. Use reload to reset to defaults.*

systemctl start firewalld  
*\# Start the firewalld service.*

firewall-cmd \--runtime-to-permanent  
*\# Save current runtime rules to permanent configuration storage.*  
firewall-cmd \--reload  
*\# Load saved permanent rules into the active runtime.*

systemctl enable firewalld  
*\# Ensure the service starts automatically at system boot.*

[image6]: <data:image/png;base64,>

[image7]: <data:image/png;base64,>

[image8]: <data:image/png;base64,>

[image9]: <data:image/png;base64,>

[image10]: <data:image/png;base64,>

[image11]: <data:image/png;base64,>
